div.head: The Vice Consul at Jerusalem (Cobb) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
Reference capitulations: Chief Justice Palestine Government has verbally informed me that our capitulations are not in accord with the Palestine order in council (enclosure to my despatch no. 1018, September 6, 1922Not printed.) and Attorney General has verbally informed me local government cannot force him to comply with instructions of the Chief Secretary, referred to in my despatch no. 1104, March 3, 1923, though lower courts have and are continuing to do so.
Article 87 of the aforementioned order in council authorizes High Commissioner to amend order in council but for political reasons it appears inexpedient to local government lest other nations who have not successfully maintained their capitulations might be offended. It appears necessary that action to this end be taken through London.
